title: Skill Bundles (community + Hermes docs)
summary: Skill Bundles let you trigger groups of skills with one slash command via a tiny YAML file. Canonical examples: /release-prep, /incident-response, /research-sprint. Source 1tixe6k (111/12).
key_concepts: one slash command → group of skills
gotchas: u/kruzibit (+1): Skills loaded but unused burn tokens — unclear from current docs whether bundles eager-load all skills or lazy-load on demand. Verify before adding heavyweight skills to a bundle
full_text:
Source: r/hermesagent post 1tixe6k (111 score, 12 comments).

Nous Research dropped Skill Bundles — tiny YAML files that group skills behind one slash command. Canonical examples: /release-prep, /incident-response, /research-sprint.

Docs reference: hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/user-guide/features/skills#skill-bundles

Open question (u/kruzibit): eager-load all skills in a bundle vs lazy-load — token-cost implications matter for heavyweight skills.
